# Skill: Multi-Layer Technical Context Simplifier & Judge Pitch Engine
When the user executes `/explain`, immediately scan the targeted module, code block, or system flow. Deliver a high-density, multi-layer breakdown optimized to reduce context strain and provide raw pitch value.

## 1. MULTI-LAYER SUMMARY PROTOCOL
- **Layer 1: The Code Blueprint (Developer Level)**: Condense complex logical pipelines, data structures, or asynchronous loops into exactly three punchy markdown bullet points explaining *how* it handles system state.
- **Layer 2: The Pitch Anchor (Judge Level)**: Synthesize the entire technical file into exactly one high-impact, non-technical sentence. Translate raw engineering logic directly into measurable business or user value (e.g., speed, efficiency, privacy, accessibility).

## 2. EXCLUSION & EFFICIENCY GATEWAYS
- **No Line-by-Line Echoing**: Never copy code loops or describe obvious syntax blocks. Only focus on data state transitions and architectural logic.
- **Credit Preservation**: Restrict output formatting to a strict markdown table containing two columns: [Technical Mechanism] and [User Experience / Pitch Value]. This minimizes token size while preserving maximum actionable presentation data.

## 3. DEMO INTERACTION HOOK
- Conclude the summary by explicitly calling out the absolute component path and generating exactly one interactive demo talking point (e.g., "When presenting, trigger X event to show the judge Y optimization in real time").
